texto: Beryl, on death: “No, you should never choose death, but it chooses you. Sometimes you know it’s coming, like my mother, and sometimes it takes you by surprise, like my friend Ariel’s uncle who got killed in a hunting accident. Sooner or later, we all get chosen, we all get taken from this world into the next. Whether we end up in Heaven or Hell, and what we do with our lives in the meantime—those are the choices we get to make.” – The Enemy’s Table
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Charlotte Woollard: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Beryl, on death: “No, you should never choose death, but it chooses you. Sometimes you know it’s …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, choices, death pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e termino.
